items.prompt: For reproducibility, quality decisions such as imputation or exclusion should be:
choices: Documented so the published analysis can be repeated under the same conditions
Hidden to keep competitive advantage forever
Replaced by deleting all incomplete rows without a note
Ignored once FAIR Findable metadata exists
items.explain: Section 13.4.3 requires documenting quality decisions so analyses remain repeatable.

items.prompt: Documenting clinical trial dropouts and their analytical impact is an example of completeness documentation for reproducibility.
items.explain: Example 13.18 requires noting dropouts and their impact on the analysis.

items.prompt: In this part, research-facing provenance primarily records:
choices: Origin, transformations, context, and processing decisions for a dataset
items.explain: Provenance tracks where data came from, how they were collected and transformed, and related decisions.

items.prompt: Name one provenance field a genomics study should record according to Example 13.24.
items.rubric: Accept sequencing technology, preprocessing steps, curation decisions, or equivalent.
items.sample_answer: Sequencing technology (or preprocessing steps / curation decisions).

items.prompt: Tracking dataset updates over time helps preserve integrity and lets future users understand how the dataset evolved.
items.explain: Example 13.25 frames update tracking as integrity and evolution documentation.
